summary: retention.py is well-structured with strong type hints and error handling. However, workflow has critical gaps: (1) actions/checkout@v4 not pinned to commit SHA (supply-chain risk); (2) workflow_dispatch input 'dry_run' defined but never used in cleanup-plan step; (3) workflow references tests/ and fixtures/ directories that don't exist in deliverable—unit-tests job will fail on `python3 -m unittest discover -v -s tests` if tests/ missing; cleanup-plan will fail on fixture file checks. run_act_tests.py has fragile output-format-dependent assertions and confusing job-success detection logic (lines 129-133). Without actual test files and fixture data, cannot verify tests run or pass. Workflow structure is sound but incomplete—missing .actrc for act image config and integration point to run the harness itself.
